rules.rule: Every engineering team must define and document an Overall Purpose covering Business Nature, Business Goals, and Future Considerations.
Business Nature must state what the business is in plain, non-technical language.
Business Goals must state what the business aims to deliver to its customers.
Future Considerations must state the anticipated growth or evolution of the business scope.
Scenarios must be written in BDD format using exactly three clauses: GIVEN (actor), WHEN (interaction), THEN (outcome).
Scenarios must be non-technical and written from the end-user's perspective.
Scenario outcomes (THEN) must express results that could be advertised to non-technical stakeholders.
Chained scenarios must explicitly reference the prior scenario they build on.
Scenarios must not contain API endpoints, HTTP status codes, database terms, or UI implementation details unless the business itself is technical.
A purpose document must not omit any of the three required sections: Business Nature, Business Goals, Future Considerations.
A scenario must not omit the actor (GIVEN), the interaction (WHEN), or the outcome (THEN).
